351	    try:
352	        validate(ref)
353	        rep.ok("cli-reference.json valid vs schema (source + provenance on every entry)")
354	    except jsonschema.ValidationError as exc:
355	        rep.fail(f"cli-reference.json invalid vs schema: {exc.message}")
356	
357	    current_md = REF_MD.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if REF_MD.exists() else None
358	    rep.check(current_md == render_page(ref),
359	              "cli-reference.md in sync with cli-reference.json",
360	              "cli-reference.md out of sync — run `tools/render-cli-reference --render`")
361	
362	    if with_cli:
363	        try:
364	            fresh = emit(build_reference())
365	        except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.SubprocessError, SystemExit) as exc:
366	            rep.pending(f"--cli machine-freshness skipped — CLI unavailable ({exc})")
367	            return
368	        rep.check(fresh == committed_json,
369	                  "cli-reference.json matches the installed CLI",
370	                  "cli-reference.json stale vs installed CLI — run `tools/render-cli-reference --all`")

375	    do_generate = "--generate" in argv or "--all" in argv
376	    do_render = "--render" in argv or "--all" in argv

378	    do_check = "--check" in argv or not (do_generate or do_render)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""render-cli-reference: build and render the exhaustive, version-resilient CLI reference (R16).

One tool, explicit modes, so the EXPENSIVE recursive `claude --help` introspection is isolated
from the pure/offline render and never duplicated (design §12.1):

  --generate   Introspect `claude --help` recursively ∪ the authored supplement ->
               meta/cli-reference.json (the authoritative machine artifact). Needs the CLI.
               Also delta-marks new commands/flags vs the prior committed json (`added_in`, §12.4).
  --render     Read the json + meta/version-changelog.md -> course/reference/cli-reference.md. Offline.
  --all        --generate then --render (the drift/refresh entrypoint).
  --check      (default) Offline freshness/validity gate for `make check`; `--check --cli` also
               re-introspects the CLI and diffs the json (machine freshness).

The machine artifact is BYTE-STABLE: it carries `cli_version` but no generated-date (that lives in
meta/version-record.md), and emits with sorted keys + preserved list order, so the same CLI + same
supplement + same prior-json produce identical bytes — which is what makes the regenerate-and-diff
freshness check meaningful (R16.AC6). The page additionally surfaces the latest version-changelog
entry as a "What's new" section (R17 learner surface, design §12.5).
"""
import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
from _common import (  # noqa: E402
    COURSE, META, Reporter, cli_version, introspect_cli, load_version_data, load_yaml, render_vd,
)

REF_JSON = META / "cli-reference.json"
SUPPLEMENT = META / "cli-reference-supplement.yaml"
SCHEMA = META / "cli-reference.schema.json"
REF_MD = COURSE / "reference" / "cli-reference.md"
VERSION_CHANGELOG = META / "version-changelog.md"

SCHEMA_VERSION = 1

MD_HEADER = (
    "<!-- GENERATED from meta/cli-reference.json by tools/render-cli-reference — do not hand-edit. "
    "Run `tools/render-cli-reference --all` (or `make render`). -->\n"
)
# Up-navigation, like the units' breadcrumb (course/reference/cli-reference.md -> repo root).
MD_BREADCRUMB = "[Claude Code Mastery](../../README.md) › CLI reference\n"


def build_supplement_sections():
    """Load the authored supplement, stamping `source: supplement` and inheriting each section's
    provenance onto entries that don't carry their own (R16.AC3/AC4)."""
    data = load_yaml(SUPPLEMENT) or {}
    sections = []
    for sec in data.get("sections", []):
        prov = sec["provenance"]
        entries = []
        for e in sec.get("entries", []):
            entry = {
                "name": e["name"],
                "description": e.get("description", ""),
                "source": "supplement",
                "provenance": e.get("provenance", prov),
            }
            if e.get("unverified"):
                entry["unverified"] = True
            entries.append(entry)
        section = {"title": sec["title"], "provenance": prov, "entries": entries}
        if sec.get("note"):
            section["note"] = sec["note"]
        sections.append(section)
    return sections


def build_reference():
    """Assemble the full machine artifact (introspected CLI tree ∪ authored supplement)."""
    version = cli_version()
    if not version:
        raise SystemExit("render-cli-reference: could not read `claude --version`")
    root = introspect_cli()
    prior = json.loads(REF_JSON.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if REF_JSON.exists() else None
    if prior:
        _mark_added_in(root, prior.get("root"), prior.get("cli_version"), version)
    return {
        "schema_version": SCHEMA_VERSION,
        "cli_version": version,
        "root": root,
        "supplement_sections": build_supplement_sections(),
    }


# --- added_in delta (R16; design §12.4 step 4) ----------------------------------------------
#
# Mark commands/flags that newly appeared versus the previously-committed reference, so a new
# option is flagged inline (§12.5). Verified by construction (a real introspected-surface delta,
# not memory) and byte-stable: regenerating at the same version diffs against the current committed
# json — so nothing reads as new, and existing markers are carried forward unchanged.

def _index_tree(node, cmds, flags):
    cmds[tuple(node["path"])] = node
    for f in node["flags"]:
        flags[(tuple(node["path"]), tuple(f["names"]))] = f
    for child in node["commands"]:
        _index_tree(child, cmds, flags)


def _mark_added_in(root, prior_root, prior_version, version):
    if not prior_root:
        return  # first generation: no prior surface to diff against
    bumped = bool(prior_version) and prior_version != version
    pcmds, pflags = {}, {}
    _index_tree(prior_root, pcmds, pflags)

    def walk(node):
        if node["path"]:  # never mark the root command itself
            prior = pcmds.get(tuple(node["path"]))
            if prior and prior.get("added_in"):
                node["added_in"] = prior["added_in"]   # carry forward
            elif prior is None and bumped:
                node["added_in"] = version             # newly appeared this bump
        for f in node["flags"]:
            prior = pflags.get((tuple(node["path"]), tuple(f["names"])))
            if prior and prior.get("added_in"):
                f["added_in"] = prior["added_in"]
            elif prior is None and bumped:
                f["added_in"] = version
        for child in node["commands"]:
            walk(child)

    walk(root)


def emit(obj):
    """Byte-stable JSON: sorted keys, preserved list order, no generated-date (R16.AC6)."""
    return json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True) + "\n"


def validate(obj):
    """Validate the artifact against the committed JSON Schema (raises on failure)."""
    import jsonschema  # local import: only --generate/--check need it
    jsonschema.validate(obj, json.loads(SCHEMA.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))


def generate(rep):
    ref = build_reference()
    validate(ref)
    REF_JSON.write_text(emit(ref), encoding="utf-8")
    n_cmds = _count_commands(ref["root"])
    n_supp = sum(len(s["entries"]) for s in ref["supplement_sections"])
    rep.ok(f"generated {REF_JSON.relative_to(META.parent)} @ CLI {ref['cli_version']} "
           f"({n_cmds} command(s), {n_supp} supplement entr(ies))")


def _count_commands(node):
    return 1 + sum(_count_commands(c) for c in node["commands"])


# --- human render (R16.AC2 + R17 surface; §12.5) ---------------------------------------------
#
# Pure function of the committed sources (cli-reference.json + version-changelog.md): the page
# never re-introspects the CLI. CommonMark only (R15).

def _slug(text):
    """GitHub-style heading anchor: lowercase, drop punctuation/backticks, spaces -> hyphens."""
    s = re.sub(r"[`*_]", "", text.strip().lower())
    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", "", s)
    return re.sub(r"\s+", "-", s)


def _cell(text):
    """Escape a value for a Markdown table cell (pipes would split the row)."""
    return text.replace("|", "\\|").strip()


def _cmd_label(node):
    return "claude" if not node["path"] else "claude " + " ".join(node["path"])


MARKER = "🆕"  # always paired with the words "new in <ver>" so meaning isn't emoji-only (R15)


def _added_marker(added_in):
    return f"  {MARKER} _new in {added_in}_" if added_in else ""


def _flag_row(f):
    names = ", ".join(f"`{n}`" for n in f["names"])
    arg = f"`{f['arg']}`" if f["arg"] else "—"
    desc = _cell(f["description"])
    extra = []
    if f["choices"]:
        extra.append("choices: " + ", ".join(f"`{c}`" for c in f["choices"]))
    if f["default"] is not None:
        extra.append(f"default: `{f['default']}`")
    if extra:
        joined = "; ".join(extra)
        desc = f"{desc} — {joined}" if desc else joined
    desc += _added_marker(f.get("added_in"))
    return f"| {names} | {arg} | {desc} |"


def _whats_new_section():
    """The R17 learner surface (§12.5): the latest version-changelog.md entry, {{vd:key}}-resolved,
    plus a link to the full digest. Empty if the changelog is absent.

    Fence-aware: the digest documents an example entry inside a ``` block, so `## ` headings inside
    code fences are ignored — only real entry headings count.
    """
    if not VERSION_CHANGELOG.exists():
        return []
    resolved, _ = render_vd(VERSION_CHANGELOG.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), load_version_data())
    body = resolved.splitlines()
    heads, fence = [], False
    for i, line in enumerate(body):
        if line.lstrip().startswith("```"):
            fence = not fence
        elif not fence and line.startswith("## "):
            heads.append(i)
    if not heads:
        return []
    start = heads[0]
    end = heads[1] if len(heads) > 1 else len(body)
    out = [
        "## What's new",
        "",
        "Latest CLI changes — full history in "
        "[meta/version-changelog.md](../../meta/version-changelog.md).",
        "",
        f"**{body[start][3:].strip()}**",
        "",
    ]
    entry = [l.rstrip() for l in body[start + 1:end]]
    while entry and not entry[0]:        # drop the blank line(s) right under the heading
        entry.pop(0)
    while entry and not entry[-1]:       # and any trailing blanks before the next entry
        entry.pop()
    return out + entry


def _render_command(node, lines):
    """Nested command section: heading depth = command depth; flag/argument tables."""
    lines.append("#" * min(2 + len(node["path"]), 6)
                 + f" `{_cmd_label(node)}`" + _added_marker(node.get("added_in")))
    lines.append("")
    if node["description"]:
        lines.append(_cell(node["description"]))
        lines.append("")
    if node["usage"]:
        lines.append(f"**Usage:** `{node['usage']}`")
        lines.append("")
    if node["arguments"]:
        lines += ["| Argument | Description |", "| --- | --- |"]
        for a in node["arguments"]:
            lines.append(f"| `{_cell(a['name'])}` | {_cell(a['description'])} |")
        lines.append("")
    if node["flags"]:
        lines += ["| Flag | Value | Description |", "| --- | --- | --- |"]
        lines += [_flag_row(f) for f in node["flags"]]
        lines.append("")
    for child in node["commands"]:
        _render_command(child, lines)


def _render_supplement(sec, lines):
    lines.append(f"## {sec['title']}")
    lines.append("")
    url = re.search(r"https?://\S+", sec["provenance"])
    prov = sec["provenance"]
    if url:
        prov = prov.replace(url.group(0), f"[{url.group(0)}]({url.group(0)})")
    lines.append(f"_Doc-sourced — {prov}_")
    lines.append("")
    if sec.get("note"):
        lines.append(_cell(sec["note"]))
        lines.append("")
    lines += ["| Name | Description |", "| --- | --- |"]
    for e in sec["entries"]:
        name = _cell(e["name"])
        name_cell = f"`{name}`" if e["name"].startswith("/") else f"**{name}**"
        desc = _cell(e["description"])
        if e.get("unverified"):
            desc = f"{desc} _(unverified — see provenance)_".strip()
        lines.append(f"| {name_cell} | {desc} |")
    lines.append("")


def _render_toc(ref, lines):
    lines += ["## Contents", ""]
    if VERSION_CHANGELOG.exists():
        lines.append("- [What's new](#whats-new)")

    def walk(node):
        lines.append("  " * len(node["path"]) + f"- [`{_cmd_label(node)}`](#{_slug(_cmd_label(node))})")
        for child in node["commands"]:
            walk(child)

    walk(ref["root"])
    for sec in ref["supplement_sections"]:
        lines.append(f"- [{sec['title']}](#{_slug(sec['title'])})")
    lines.append("")


def render_page(ref):
    lines = [
        "# Claude Code CLI reference",
        "",
        "> ⚙️ **Generated** from `meta/cli-reference.json` by `tools/render-cli-reference` — do not "
        "edit by hand; regenerate with `tools/render-cli-reference --all`.",
        ">",
        f"> Reflects Claude Code CLI **{ref['cli_version']}**.",
        "",
        "The command tree, flags, and arguments below are introspected directly from `claude --help` "
        "(recursively over subcommands). The final sections cover doc-only surface `--help` cannot "
        "see — in-REPL slash commands and output styles — sourced from the official docs, with the "
        "URL and retrieval date shown.",
        "",
    ]
    _render_toc(ref, lines)
    whats_new = _whats_new_section()
    if whats_new:
        lines += whats_new + [""]
    _render_command(ref["root"], lines)
    for sec in ref["supplement_sections"]:
        _render_supplement(sec, lines)
    return MD_HEADER + MD_BREADCRUMB + "\n" + "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"


def render(rep):
    if not REF_JSON.exists():
        rep.fail(f"{REF_JSON.name} missing — run `--generate` first")
        return
    ref = json.loads(REF_JSON.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    REF_MD.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    REF_MD.write_text(render_page(ref), encoding="utf-8")
    rep.ok(f"rendered {REF_MD.relative_to(COURSE.parent)} @ CLI {ref['cli_version']}")


def check(rep, with_cli=False):
    """Offline freshness/validity gate for `make check` (R16.AC6).

    Pure functions of the committed artifacts: (1) the json validates against the schema — which
    requires `source` + `provenance` on every entry (R16.AC3); (2) the committed page matches a
    fresh render of the json. With `--cli` it ALSO re-introspects the installed CLI and diffs the
    json (machine freshness) — the refresh/strict-only path, since that step needs the CLI.
    """
    if not REF_JSON.exists():
        rep.fail(f"{REF_JSON.name} missing — run `tools/render-cli-reference --generate`")
        return
    committed_json = REF_JSON.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    ref = json.loads(committed_json)

    import jsonschema  # local: only --check/--generate need it
    try:
        validate(ref)
        rep.ok("cli-reference.json valid vs schema (source + provenance on every entry)")
    except jsonschema.ValidationError as exc:
        rep.fail(f"cli-reference.json invalid vs schema: {exc.message}")

    current_md = REF_MD.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if REF_MD.exists() else None
    rep.check(current_md == render_page(ref),
              "cli-reference.md in sync with cli-reference.json",
              "cli-reference.md out of sync — run `tools/render-cli-reference --render`")

    if with_cli:
        try:
            fresh = emit(build_reference())
        except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.SubprocessError, SystemExit) as exc:
            rep.pending(f"--cli machine-freshness skipped — CLI unavailable ({exc})")
            return
        rep.check(fresh == committed_json,
                  "cli-reference.json matches the installed CLI",
                  "cli-reference.json stale vs installed CLI — run `tools/render-cli-reference --all`")


def main(argv):
    rep = Reporter("render-cli-reference")
    do_generate = "--generate" in argv or "--all" in argv
    do_render = "--render" in argv or "--all" in argv
    # --check is the default when no build mode is given (design §12.1).
    do_check = "--check" in argv or not (do_generate or do_render)
    if do_generate:
        generate(rep)
    if do_render:
        render(rep)
    if do_check:
        check(rep, with_cli="--cli" in argv)
    return rep.done()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

1	# Version record
2	
3	The CLI version the course content was **last verified against**, and how. Bumped on every
4	refresh (R12.AC5/AC7). The drift check (`tools/check-version-drift`) compares the installed
5	`claude --version` to the top row here and prompts a refresh when they differ.
6	
7	The course is **latest-targeting but verified-anchored** (R12.AC1): it tracks the current CLI
8	and is not hard-pinned, but every version-specific value in `meta/version-data.yaml` carries its
9	own `verified_version` + `verified_date` + `provenance`.
10	
11	| Verified CLI version | Date | Method | By |
12	|---|---|---|---|
13	| 2.1.158 | 2026-05-30 | in-REPL `/help` + docs pass (L1 close): 5 of 7 `unverified` keys confirmed — `search-refs`, `context-cmds`, `checkpoint-rewind` (rewind = `/rewind`), `test-run`, `output-styles`; `ci` + `managed-settings` stay `unverified` (no GitHub Actions / enterprise account to verify against); also confirmed `review-cmds` slash names (`/code-review`, `/security-review`) in-REPL | L1 verification |
14	| 2.1.158 | 2026-05-29 | `claude --version` + `claude --help` (P2 version-data seeding); 19/26 keys confirmed from CLI help, 7 marked `unverified` pending in-REPL `/help` / docs | spec build (P2) |
15	
16	## How to refresh (R12.AC7)
17	
18	1. Run `tools/check-version-drift` (or just `claude --version`) and compare to the top row above.
19	2. For each changed area, re-verify the affected `{{vd:key}}` values against the installed CLI
20	   (`claude --help`, subcommand `--help`, in-REPL `/help`, official docs). **Never** copy a value
21	   from memory (R12.AC3).
22	3. Update **only** `meta/version-data.yaml` (then regenerate `meta/version-data.json`) plus any
23	   genuinely affected location. Because version-specifics are quarantined, prose rarely changes
24	   (R12.AC8).
25	4. **Regenerate the CLI reference (R16.AC5):** `tools/render-cli-reference --all` — re-introspects the
26	   installed CLI into `meta/cli-reference.json` and re-renders `course/reference/cli-reference.md`.
27	   Newly-added commands/flags are auto-flagged `added_in` against the prior committed reference.
28	5. **Record the change synopsis (R17):** add one cumulative entry at the top of
29	   `meta/version-changelog.md` covering every version strictly after the last recorded through the new
30	   one — with the official changelog/release-notes URL + retrieval date, flagging content-affecting
31	   changes (new/removed/renamed commands or flags, changed defaults, deprecations). This is also
32	   surfaced learner-facing as the page's "What's new" section.
33	6. Add a new row at the top of the table above with the new version, date, and method.
34	7. Re-run the enforcement suite (`tools/check-version-refs`, `tools/check-version-drift`,
35	   `tools/render-cli-reference --check`, `tools/check-version-changelog`, …).
36	
37	## Outstanding to verify on next refresh
38	
39	> Canonical open-loop is **L1** in `specs/claude-code-mastery/decisions.md` → "Open loops &
40	> deferrals 🔓". This list is the local detail for that loop.
41	
42	As of the 2026-05-30 L1 pass, 5 of the original 7 are verified and flipped to `unverified: false`
43	(`search-refs`, `context-cmds`, `checkpoint-rewind`, `test-run`, `output-styles`). Two remain
44	`unverified: true` in `meta/version-data.yaml`, each blocked by access this environment lacks:
45	
46	- `ci` — the CLI flags (`-p`, `--output-format`, `--max-budget-usd`) are verified; the **GitHub Action
47	  wrapper** is not (the maintainer's env uses Gitea, with no GitHub Actions to verify against). Confirm
48	  the Action name/setup against official docs in an environment with GitHub Actions.
49	- `managed-settings` — the enterprise/managed-settings **file path + precedence** needs an enterprise
50	  account or official enterprise docs (not available to the maintainer).
51

# Version record

The CLI version the course content was **last verified against**, and how. Bumped on every
refresh (R12.AC5/AC7). The drift check (`tools/check-version-drift`) compares the installed
`claude --version` to the top row here and prompts a refresh when they differ.

The course is **latest-targeting but verified-anchored** (R12.AC1): it tracks the current CLI
and is not hard-pinned, but every version-specific value in `meta/version-data.yaml` carries its
own `verified_version` + `verified_date` + `provenance`.

| Verified CLI version | Date | Method | By |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2.1.158 | 2026-05-30 | in-REPL `/help` + docs pass (L1 close): 5 of 7 `unverified` keys confirmed — `search-refs`, `context-cmds`, `checkpoint-rewind` (rewind = `/rewind`), `test-run`, `output-styles`; `ci` + `managed-settings` stay `unverified` (no GitHub Actions / enterprise account to verify against); also confirmed `review-cmds` slash names (`/code-review`, `/security-review`) in-REPL | L1 verification |
| 2.1.158 | 2026-05-29 | `claude --version` + `claude --help` (P2 version-data seeding); 19/26 keys confirmed from CLI help, 7 marked `unverified` pending in-REPL `/help` / docs | spec build (P2) |

## How to refresh (R12.AC7)

1. Run `tools/check-version-drift` (or just `claude --version`) and compare to the top row above.
2. For each changed area, re-verify the affected `{{vd:key}}` values against the installed CLI
   (`claude --help`, subcommand `--help`, in-REPL `/help`, official docs). **Never** copy a value
   from memory (R12.AC3).
3. Update **only** `meta/version-data.yaml` (then regenerate `meta/version-data.json`) plus any
   genuinely affected location. Because version-specifics are quarantined, prose rarely changes
   (R12.AC8).
4. **Regenerate the CLI reference (R16.AC5):** `tools/render-cli-reference --all` — re-introspects the
   installed CLI into `meta/cli-reference.json` and re-renders `course/reference/cli-reference.md`.
   Newly-added commands/flags are auto-flagged `added_in` against the prior committed reference.
5. **Record the change synopsis (R17):** add one cumulative entry at the top of
   `meta/version-changelog.md` covering every version strictly after the last recorded through the new
   one — with the official changelog/release-notes URL + retrieval date, flagging content-affecting
   changes (new/removed/renamed commands or flags, changed defaults, deprecations). This is also
   surfaced learner-facing as the page's "What's new" section.
6. Add a new row at the top of the table above with the new version, date, and method.
7. Re-run the enforcement suite (`tools/check-version-refs`, `tools/check-version-drift`,
   `tools/render-cli-reference --check`, `tools/check-version-changelog`, …).

## Outstanding to verify on next refresh

> Canonical open-loop is **L1** in `specs/claude-code-mastery/decisions.md` → "Open loops &
> deferrals 🔓". This list is the local detail for that loop.

As of the 2026-05-30 L1 pass, 5 of the original 7 are verified and flipped to `unverified: false`
(`search-refs`, `context-cmds`, `checkpoint-rewind`, `test-run`, `output-styles`). Two remain
`unverified: true` in `meta/version-data.yaml`, each blocked by access this environment lacks:

- `ci` — the CLI flags (`-p`, `--output-format`, `--max-budget-usd`) are verified; the **GitHub Action
  wrapper** is not (the maintainer's env uses Gitea, with no GitHub Actions to verify against). Confirm
  the Action name/setup against official docs in an environment with GitHub Actions.
- `managed-settings` — the enterprise/managed-settings **file path + precedence** needs an enterprise
  account or official enterprise docs (not available to the maintainer).

content.content:
1	"""Shared helpers for the course's enforcement suite.
2	
3	Every check imports from here so the tools stay small and consistent — they are meant to be
4	read verbatim as the worked examples that units U14 (hooks) and U16 (CI) reference (R14.AC2/AC3),
5	so clarity matters as much as correctness.
6	
7	Design notes:
8	- The repo root is found by walking up to the directory that contains `meta/` and `course/`.
9	- Meaning is carried by text ("PASS"/"FAIL"/"PENDING"), never by colour alone (R15.AC6); colour
10	  is added only when stdout is a TTY, as decoration.
11	- Version-specific values live solely in meta/version-data.yaml and are referenced as {{vd:key}};
12	  `resolve_vd` / `render_vd` implement that single-source rule (R12.AC2).
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import os
17	import re
18	import subprocess
19	import sys
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	import yaml
23	
24	
25	# --- repo layout -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	def repo_root() -> Path:
28	    here = Path(__file__).resolve()
29	    for parent in [here.parent, *here.parents]:
30	        if (parent / "meta").is_dir() and (parent / "course").is_dir():
31	            return parent
32	    raise SystemExit("could not locate repo root (no dir with meta/ and course/)")
33	
34	
35	ROOT = repo_root()
36	META = ROOT / "meta"
37	COURSE = ROOT / "course"
38	UNITS = COURSE / "units"
39	
40	# Directories scanned for course artifacts (excludes the spec, tooling internals, vendored dirs).
41	SKIP_DIRS = {".git", ".venv", "venv", "__pycache__", "node_modules", ".pytest_cache"}
42	
43	
44	def load_yaml(path: Path):
45	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
46	        return yaml.safe_load(fh)
47	
48	
49	def iter_markdown(*roots: Path):
50	    """Yield every .md file under the given roots, skipping vendored/generated dirs."""
51	    for root in roots:
52	        if not root.exists():
53	            continue
54	        for path in sorted(root.rglob("*.md")):
55	            if any(part in SKIP_DIRS for part in path.parts):
56	                continue
57	            yield path
58	
59	
60	def iter_text_files(*roots: Path):
61	    """Yield text-ish files (md/yaml/json/py/sh/toml/yml/cfg/ini) under roots."""
62	    exts = {".md", ".yaml", ".yml", ".json", ".py", ".sh", ".toml", ".cfg", ".ini", ".txt"}
63	    for root in roots:
64	        if not root.exists():
65	            continue
66	        for path in sorted(root.rglob("*")):
67	            if path.is_dir() or any(part in SKIP_DIRS for part in path.parts):
68	                continue
69	            if path.suffix in exts or os.access(path, os.X_OK):
70	                yield path
71	
72	
73	# --- unit front matter -----------------------------------------------------------------------
74	
75	FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(r"^---\n(.*?)\n---\n", re.DOTALL)
76	
77	
78	def parse_frontmatter(path: Path):
79	    """Return the parsed YAML front-matter dict for a unit file, or None if absent."""
80	    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
81	    # Allow a leading HTML comment block (templates) before the front matter.
82	    text = re.sub(r"^<!--.*?-->\s*", "", text, count=1, flags=re.DOTALL)
83	    m = FRONTMATTER_RE.match(text)
84	    if not m:
85	        return None
86	    return yaml.safe_load(m.group(1))
87	
88	
89	def unit_files():
90	    """Authored unit files: course/units/NN-slug/unit.md (excludes templates in meta/)."""
91	    if not UNITS.exists():
92	        return []
93	    return sorted(UNITS.glob("*/unit.md"))
94	
95	
96	# --- version-data resolution ({{vd:key}}) ----------------------------------------------------
97	
98	VD_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{vd:([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)\}\}")
99	
100	
101	def load_version_data():
102	    return load_yaml(META / "version-data.yaml")
103	
104	
105	def resolve_vd(key: str, vd: dict):
106	    """Resolve a single {{vd:key}}. Returns (value, unverified) or (None, None) if unknown.
107	
108	    Keys starting with '_' (e.g. _verified_version) come from the file's top level; all others
109	    come from the `keys:` table.
110	    """
111	    if key.startswith("_"):
112	        val = vd.get(key)
113	        return (val, False) if val is not None else (None, None)
114	    entry = (vd.get("keys") or {}).get(key)
115	    if entry is None:
116	        return (None, None)
117	    return (entry.get("value", ""), bool(entry.get("unverified")))
118	
119	
120	def render_vd(text: str, vd: dict):
121	    """Render every {{vd:key}} in `text`. Returns (rendered_text, unresolved_keys:set).
122	
123	    Unverified values are annotated inline with an explicit text marker (R12.AC3).
124	    """
125	    unresolved: set[str] = set()
126	
127	    def repl(match: re.Match) -> str:
128	        key = match.group(1)
129	        value, unverified = resolve_vd(key, vd)
130	        if value is None:
131	            unresolved.add(key)
132	            return match.group(0)  # leave token in place; caller decides how to fail
133	        return f"{value} (unverified — see meta/version-record.md)" if unverified else value
134	
135	    return VD_TOKEN_RE.sub(repl, text), unresolved
136	
137	
138	# --- CLI help introspection (R16; §12.4) -----------------------------------------------------
139	#
140	# These helpers turn `claude --help` (recursively, over subcommands) into a structured command
141	# tree, so the exhaustive CLI reference (meta/cli-reference.json) is *generated from the installed
142	# CLI*, never authored from memory (R12.AC3). They are deliberately tolerant of help-shape drift:
143	# a line or section that doesn't parse is skipped rather than fatal, so a future CLI whose help
144	# format shifts still yields a usable (if coarser) reference — and trips the freshness check.
145	
146	CLI = "claude"
147	HELP_TIMEOUT = 30          # seconds per `claude … --help` call
148	HELP_MAX_DEPTH = 3         # how deep to recurse into subcommands
149	
150	_SECTION_RE = re.compile(r"^(Usage|Arguments|Options|Commands):\s*(.*)$")
151	_ENTRY_START_RE = re.compile(r"^ {2}\S")      # a list entry begins at exactly two spaces
152	_CONT_RE = re.compile(r"^ {3,}\S")            # wrapped/continuation lines are indented deeper
153	_COMMAND_HEAD_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-z][\w-]*(\|[a-z][\w-]*)*$")
154	
155	
156	def run_help(path, timeout=HELP_TIMEOUT):
157	    """Return stdout of `claude <path…> --help` (path = [] for the root command)."""
158	    out = subprocess.run([CLI, *path, "--help"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout)
159	    return out.stdout
160	
161	
162	def cli_version(timeout=HELP_TIMEOUT):
163	    """Return the installed CLI version (e.g. '2.1.159'), or None if unparseable."""
164	    out = subprocess.run([CLI, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout)
165	    m = re.search(r"\d+\.\d+\.\d+", out.stdout)
166	    return m.group(0) if m else None
167	
168	
169	def _group_entries(lines):
170	    """Group a help section's raw lines into entries (each a list of its own raw lines).
171	
172	    An entry starts at a two-space indent; more-deeply-indented lines (wrapped descriptions,
173	    whether at the description column or the six-space overflow column) belong to it.
174	    """
175	    entries, cur = [], None
176	    for line in lines:
177	        if not line.strip():
178	            continue
179	        if _ENTRY_START_RE.match(line):
180	            if cur is not None:
181	                entries.append(cur)
182	            cur = [line]
183	        elif cur is not None and _CONT_RE.match(line):
184	            cur.append(line)
185	    if cur is not None:
186	        entries.append(cur)
187	    return entries
188	
189	
190	def _term_and_desc(entry_lines):
191	    """Split one entry into its term (left column) and joined description (right column).
192	
193	    The term and description on the first line are separated by ≥2 spaces; when the term is too
194	    long, the description starts on the (more-indented) continuation lines instead.
195	    """
196	    parts = re.split(r"\s{2,}", entry_lines[0].strip(), maxsplit=1)
197	    desc = [parts[1]] if len(parts) == 2 else []
198	    desc += [c.strip() for c in entry_lines[1:]]
199	    return parts[0], " ".join(desc).strip()
200	
201	
202	def parse_flag_term(term):
203	    """`-p, --print` → (['-p','--print'], None); `--add-dir <dirs...>` → (['--add-dir'], '<dirs...>')."""
204	    arg = None
205	    m = re.search(r"\s([<\[].*[>\]])$", term)
206	    if m:
207	        arg, term = m.group(1), term[:m.start()].rstrip()
208	    names = [t.strip() for t in term.split(",") if t.strip()]
209	    return names, arg
210	
211	
212	def _split_choices_default(desc):
213	    """Lift a `(choices: …)` / `(default: …)` parenthetical out of a description into fields."""
214	    choices = default = None
215	    cm = re.search(r"\(choices:\s*(.+?)\)", desc)
216	    if cm:
217	        inner = re.split(r",\s*(?:preset|default):", cm.group(1))[0]
218	        choices = re.findall(r'"([^"]*)"', inner) or None
219	        desc = (desc[:cm.start()] + desc[cm.end():]).strip()
220	    dm = re.search(r"\(default:\s*(.+?)\)", desc)
221	    if dm:
222	        default = dm.group(1).strip()
223	        desc = (desc[:dm.start()] + desc[dm.end():]).strip()
224	    return re.sub(r"\s{2,}", " ", desc).strip(), choices, default
225	
226	
227	def parse_help(text):
228	    """Parse one `claude … --help` page into {usage, description, arguments, flags, commands}.
229	
230	    Pure: it stamps no source/provenance (the introspection layer does that) and tolerates
231	    unrecognised lines (e.g. embedded example blocks) by skipping them rather than raising.
232	    """
233	    buckets = {"_desc": [], "Arguments": [], "Options": [], "Commands": []}
234	    usage, current = "", "_desc"
235	    for line in text.splitlines():
236	        m = _SECTION_RE.match(line)
237	        if m:
238	            if m.group(1) == "Usage":
239	                usage, current = m.group(2).strip(), "_desc"
240	            else:
241	                current = m.group(1)
242	            continue
243	        buckets[current].append(line)
244	
245	    description = " ".join(l.strip() for l in buckets["_desc"] if l.strip())
246	
247	    arguments = []
248	    for entry in _group_entries(buckets["Arguments"]):
249	        name, desc = _term_and_desc(entry)
250	        arguments.append({"name": name, "description": desc})
251	
252	    flags = []
253	    for entry in _group_entries(buckets["Options"]):
254	        term, desc = _term_and_desc(entry)
255	        if not term.startswith("-"):
256	            continue  # defensive: only real flags belong under Options
257	        names, arg = parse_flag_term(term)
258	        desc, choices, default = _split_choices_default(desc)
259	        flags.append({"names": names, "arg": arg, "description": desc,
260	                      "choices": choices, "default": default})
261	
262	    commands = []
263	    for entry in _group_entries(buckets["Commands"]):
264	        term, desc = _term_and_desc(entry)
265	        head = term.split(" ", 1)[0]
266	        if not _COMMAND_HEAD_RE.match(head):
267	            continue  # skip example blocks / stray lines masquerading as command entries
268	        commands.append({"name": head.split("|")[0], "args": term[len(head):].strip(),
269	                         "description": desc})
270	
271	    return {"usage": usage, "description": description,
272	            "arguments": arguments, "flags": flags, "commands": commands}
273	
274	
275	def introspect_cli(max_depth=HELP_MAX_DEPTH, timeout=HELP_TIMEOUT):
276	    """Recursively introspect the installed CLI into a command-tree node (the reference `root`).
277	
278	    Each node carries source/provenance (R16.AC3); subcommands are walked via `claude <path…>
279	    --help`, depth-capped, skipping the `help` pseudo-command, with a per-call timeout. A call
280	    that fails or whose help doesn't parse degrades to a minimal node rather than aborting the sweep.
281	    """
282	    return _introspect([], None, max_depth, timeout, 0)
283	
284	
285	def _introspect(path, listing_desc, max_depth, timeout, depth):
286	    provenance = "claude " + " ".join([*path, "--help"])
287	    node = {"name": path[-1] if path else CLI, "path": list(path),
288	            "usage": "", "description": listing_desc or "",
289	            "source": "cli-help", "provenance": provenance,
290	            "flags": [], "arguments": [], "commands": []}
291	    try:
292	        parsed = parse_help(run_help(path, timeout=timeout))
293	    except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.SubprocessError):
294	        return node  # tolerant: keep the listing-level node, recurse no further
295	    node["usage"] = parsed["usage"]
296	    if parsed["description"]:
297	        node["description"] = parsed["description"]
298	    stamp = {"source": "cli-help", "provenance": provenance}
299	    node["arguments"] = [dict(a, **stamp) for a in parsed["arguments"]]
300	    node["flags"] = [dict(f, **stamp) for f in parsed["flags"]]
301	    for cmd in parsed["commands"]:
302	        if cmd["name"] == "help":
303	            continue  # the `help` pseudo-command carries no new surface
304	        if depth < max_depth:
305	            node["commands"].append(
306	                _introspect([*path, cmd["name"]], cmd["description"], max_depth, timeout, depth + 1))
307	        else:
308	            node["commands"].append(
309	                {"name": cmd["name"], "path": [*path, cmd["name"]], "usage": "",
310	                 "description": cmd["description"], "source": "cli-help",
311	                 "provenance": provenance, "flags": [], "arguments": [], "commands": []})
312	    return node
313	
314	
315	# --- reporting -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
316	
317	_TTY = sys.stdout.isatty()
318	
319	
320	def _c(code: str, s: str) -> str:
321	    return f"\033[{code}m{s}\033[0m" if _TTY else s
322	
323	
324	class Reporter:
325	    """Tiny PASS/FAIL/PENDING reporter. `failures` drives the process exit code."""
326	
327	    def __init__(self, name: str):
328	        self.name = name
329	        self.failures = 0
330	        self.pending_count = 0
331	
332	    def ok(self, msg: str):
333	        print(f"  {_c('32', 'PASS')}  {msg}")
334	
335	    def fail(self, msg: str):
336	        self.failures += 1
337	        print(f"  {_c('31', 'FAIL')}  {msg}")
338	
339	    def pending(self, msg: str):
340	        self.pending_count += 1
341	        print(f"  {_c('33', 'PEND')}  {msg}")
342	
343	    def check(self, cond: bool, ok_msg: str, fail_msg: str | None = None):
344	        if cond:
345	            self.ok(ok_msg)
346	        else:
347	            self.fail(fail_msg or ok_msg)
348	        return cond
349	
350	    def done(self, strict: bool = False) -> int:
351	        """Print a summary and return an exit code. PENDING fails only under --strict."""
352	        bad = self.failures + (self.pending_count if strict else 0)
353	        tail = f" ({self.pending_count} pending)" if self.pending_count else ""
354	        if bad:
355	            print(f"{self.name}: {_c('31', 'FAILED')} — {self.failures} failure(s){tail}")
356	        else:
357	            print(f"{self.name}: {_c('32', 'OK')}{tail}")
358	        return 1 if bad else 0
359

message.content: In this session and the previous session, with low context pressure, you have started doing work in parallel that have race conditions with eachother. You asked to run a command that redirects output to a file at the same time you asked to read that file. This is new but consistent behavior that surfaced once early in the weekend, but resurfaced worse now and in the previous session. This implies that something in the project or local machine memory that is corrupting us, or the tool/os itself is in a bad state.

content.text:
You've made a sharp and fair diagnosis. Let me own the part that's mine before I investigate the environment, because being honest about it matters for the fix.

**What I actually did wrong** (visible in the transcript above):
1. I issued `... > /tmp/drift.txt` and `Read /tmp/drift.txt` *in the same parallel batch* — a write→read dependency executed concurrently. That's a textbook race, and it directly violates the system rule "if there are dependencies between the calls… you MUST wait for previous calls to finish." It also violates my own saved memory (`no /tmp round-trips`).
2. I fired the *same* `make drift` command 3 times and the *same* `Read` 4–5 times in single batches — because I misread "no results yet" (results in a batch only return together, at the end) as "the call returned nothing," and compensated by piling on duplicates.

So the proximate cause is a reasoning error in how I batch. But your instinct that *something is making it worse* is worth checking, and I already see one concrete environmental contradiction: **CLAUDE.md says the venv is at `.venv`, but my saved memory says it's at `/home/jj/venvs/main`.** My `.venv/bin/python` call failed with exit 127 — and *that* failure is what triggered the /tmp-round-trip scramble. A stale/contradictory memory plausibly is part of the "corruption."

Let me investigate read-only, modeling the correct discipline (these are genuinely independent reads, so batching them is correct — that's the distinction I got wrong before).

1	---
2	name: feedback-small-tool-batches
3	description: "Console \"flakiness\" was self-inflicted by oversized/duplicate tool batches — keep batches small, no duplicates, prefer dedicated tools"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: feedback
7	  originSessionId: a2724d32-ece9-4efe-a3e3-0736c86604dd
8	---
9	
10	Apparent "console flakiness" (duplicated output, dropped output, phantom lines like a stray
11	`## done (non-strict)` inside a file) is **not** an environment bug. A clean diagnostic proved
12	single, small tool calls return faithful output every time (`printf`, `Read`, `tty`, multi-file
13	`grep` all clean and exactly once).
14	
15	**Why:** the garbling came from firing huge single-message batches (10–15 tool calls), many of them
16	**exact duplicates** — the harness flagged this repeatedly with `Wasted call — file unchanged since
17	your last Read`. When many results return interleaved, output from one call bleeds into how another
18	is read, so it *looks* like corruption/duplication. The `/tmp`-redirect-then-`Read` "workaround"
19	only added more calls and noise; it fixed nothing.
20	
21	**How to apply:**
22	- One call per distinct need — never re-issue the same Read/Bash in a batch.
23	- Keep batches small and homogeneous; don't mix a dozen Reads + Bash calls in one turn.
24	- Use `Read` to read files (not `cat`/`sed`/`head`); use dedicated search/file tools over shell
25	  pipelines (`grep`/`awk`/`sed`/`echo`) — this matches the standing system-prompt guidance.
26	- Reserve `Bash` for real actions (git, make, pytest, chmod) and simple one-shot queries.
27	- Do **not** round-trip output through `/tmp`.
28	- **Before recording any "I found/changed/fixed X" claim, confirm it against a clean ground-truth
29	  read.** Phantom reads still slip through occasionally: in U4 a phantom showed a malformed
30	  `settings.json` (stray brace) that was actually valid — `json.load` even said so — and the false
31	  "fixed it" claim got propagated to four files before a clean re-read caught it. Trust the
32	  validator/ground-truth over a remembered read; never act on a single suspicious read.
33	
34	Confirmed with the user (2026-05-30) after a controlled test. Relates to [[feedback-continuity-hygiene]].
35

---
name: feedback-small-tool-batches
description: "Console \"flakiness\" was self-inflicted by oversized/duplicate tool batches — keep batches small, no duplicates, prefer dedicated tools"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: a2724d32-ece9-4efe-a3e3-0736c86604dd
---

Apparent "console flakiness" (duplicated output, dropped output, phantom lines like a stray
`## done (non-strict)` inside a file) is **not** an environment bug. A clean diagnostic proved
single, small tool calls return faithful output every time (`printf`, `Read`, `tty`, multi-file
`grep` all clean and exactly once).

**Why:** the garbling came from firing huge single-message batches (10–15 tool calls), many of them
**exact duplicates** — the harness flagged this repeatedly with `Wasted call — file unchanged since
your last Read`. When many results return interleaved, output from one call bleeds into how another
is read, so it *looks* like corruption/duplication. The `/tmp`-redirect-then-`Read` "workaround"
only added more calls and noise; it fixed nothing.

**How to apply:**
- One call per distinct need — never re-issue the same Read/Bash in a batch.
- Keep batches small and homogeneous; don't mix a dozen Reads + Bash calls in one turn.
- Use `Read` to read files (not `cat`/`sed`/`head`); use dedicated search/file tools over shell
  pipelines (`grep`/`awk`/`sed`/`echo`) — this matches the standing system-prompt guidance.
- Reserve `Bash` for real actions (git, make, pytest, chmod) and simple one-shot queries.
- Do **not** round-trip output through `/tmp`.
- **Before recording any "I found/changed/fixed X" claim, confirm it against a clean ground-truth
  read.** Phantom reads still slip through occasionally: in U4 a phantom showed a malformed
  `settings.json` (stray brace) that was actually valid — `json.load` even said so — and the false
  "fixed it" claim got propagated to four files before a clean re-read caught it. Trust the
  validator/ground-truth over a remembered read; never act on a single suspicious read.

Confirmed with the user (2026-05-30) after a controlled test. Relates to [[feedback-continuity-hygiene]].

Let me investigate the current state first (independent read-only calls — correct use of a batch).

file.content:
# Course enforcement suite. `make check` runs the required checks (R13.AC4) plus traceability
# (R13.AC5); it is the same suite wired into the git pre-commit hook and CI (R13.AC6).
#
# PY can be overridden (e.g. `make check PY=python`). The repo's virtualenv lives at .venv.

PY ?= python3
TOOLS := ./tools

.PHONY: check check-strict frontmatter coverage checklist units index links version-refs \
        cli-reference changelog traceability drift doctor render snapshot help

## Run the required + traceability checks (PENDING items do not fail; see check-strict).
check: frontmatter coverage checklist units index links version-refs cli-reference changelog traceability
	@echo "make check: all checks passed"

## Release gate: same suite, but PENDING (not-yet-authored coverage) becomes FAIL.
check-strict: frontmatter coverage checklist units index links version-refs cli-reference changelog
	@$(TOOLS)/check-traceability --strict
	@echo "make check-strict: all checks passed (strict)"

frontmatter:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-frontmatter

coverage:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-coverage

## Confirm course/progress-checklist.md is in sync with the capability map (R9.AC5).
checklist:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-checklist --check

## Confirm each learner-facing unit.md matches render(unit.src.md) — the {{vd:key}} drift gate (R12.AC2).
units:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-units --check

## Confirm the units index (course/units/README.md) is in sync with unit front matter (R9.AC2 navigation).
index:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-index --check

links:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-links

version-refs:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-version-refs

## Offline gate (R16.AC6): cli-reference.json valid vs schema + the page in sync with it.
cli-reference:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-cli-reference --check

## R17.AC5: the recorded verified version has a matching version-changelog.md entry.
changelog:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-version-changelog

traceability:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-traceability

## Drift is informational; run on demand or on a schedule (R12.AC7). Also re-introspects the
## installed CLI to confirm cli-reference.json is fresh (the --check --cli machine-freshness path).
drift:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-version-drift
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-cli-reference --check --cli

## Learner preflight (makes one real `claude -p` call unless --no-probe).
doctor:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/doctor

## Verify all {{vd:key}} references in course/ resolve; refresh generated learner docs.
render:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-vd
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-checklist
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-units
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-index
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-cli-reference --render

## Refresh the CLI command snapshot used by drift detection.
snapshot:
	@claude --help | awk '/^Commands:/{f=1;next} f && /^  [a-z]/{n=$$1; sub(/\|.*/,"",n); print n}' | sort > meta/cli-commands.snapshot
	@echo "wrote meta/cli-commands.snapshot"

help:
	@grep -B1 -E '^[a-z][a-zA-Z-]+:' Makefile | grep -E '^(##|[a-z])' | sed 'N;s/\n/ /;s/## //'

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""doctor — the course preflight (R11.AC2).
3	
4	Checks, in order, and reports pass/fail for each:
5	  1. install        — the `claude` CLI is on PATH
6	  2. version        — `claude --version` parses; compared to the recorded verified version
7	  3. auth + first   — a real non-interactive command round-trips (`claude -p`), proving auth works
8	  4. tooling deps   — the course's own check suite can run (python + pyyaml + jsonschema)
9	
10	It doubles as a worked example of *scripting Claude Code* (R14.AC2): note how step 3 drives the
11	CLI headlessly with `-p` and inspects the result — the same pattern U16 generalizes.
12	
13	Usage:
14	  tools/doctor              # run all checks (step 3 makes one real API call)
15	  tools/doctor --no-probe   # skip the API round-trip (offline / CI-safe)
16	  tools/doctor --manual     # also print the manual checklist fallback
17	"""
18	import os
19	import re
20	import shutil
21	import subprocess
22	import sys
23

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""doctor — the course preflight (R11.AC2).

Checks, in order, and reports pass/fail for each:
  1. install        — the `claude` CLI is on PATH
  2. version        — `claude --version` parses; compared to the recorded verified version
  3. auth + first   — a real non-interactive command round-trips (`claude -p`), proving auth works
  4. tooling deps   — the course's own check suite can run (python + pyyaml + jsonschema)

It doubles as a worked example of *scripting Claude Code* (R14.AC2): note how step 3 drives the
CLI headlessly with `-p` and inspects the result — the same pattern U16 generalizes.

Usage:
  tools/doctor              # run all checks (step 3 makes one real API call)
  tools/doctor --no-probe   # skip the API round-trip (offline / CI-safe)
  tools/doctor --manual     # also print the manual checklist fallback
"""
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
from _common import META, Reporter, load_version_data  # noqa: E402

MANUAL_CHECKLIST = """
Manual checklist (if the doctor can't run, or to self-verify):
  [ ] `claude --version` prints a version.
  [ ] `claude` opens an interactive session (then type /exit).
  [ ] You are authenticated (run `claude auth` if not).
  [ ] `claude -p "reply with ok"` prints a reply — proves auth + a first command.
  [ ] Python 3 is available with pyyaml + jsonschema (`make check` runs the course's own checks).
If `claude --version` differs from meta/version-record.md, see that file: some version-specific
details may have moved (they live in meta/version-data.yaml, referenced as {{vd:key}}).
""".rstrip()


def _ver_tuple(v):
    return tuple(int(x) for x in v.split("."))


def main(argv):
    rep = Reporter("doctor")
    probe = "--no-probe" not in argv
    recorded = load_version_data().get("_verified_version")

    # 1. install
    claude = shutil.which("claude")
    if not rep.check(bool(claude), f"claude is installed ({claude})", "claude not found on PATH"):
        print(MANUAL_CHECKLIST)
        return rep.done()

    # 2. version
    installed = None
    try:
        out = subprocess.run(["claude", "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
        m = re.search(r"\d+\.\d+\.\d+", out.stdout)
        installed = m.group(0) if m else None
    except (subprocess.SubprocessError, OSError) as exc:
        rep.fail(f"could not run `claude --version` ({exc})")
    if installed:
        if installed == recorded:
            rep.ok(f"version {installed} (matches recorded verified version)")
        elif _ver_tuple(installed) < _ver_tuple(recorded):
            rep.pending(f"version {installed} is older than the verified {recorded}; "
                        "some version-specific details may differ (see meta/version-record.md)")
        else:
            rep.ok(f"version {installed} (newer than verified {recorded}; latest-targeting)")
    else:
        rep.fail("could not parse `claude --version`")

    # 3. auth + first command
    if probe:
        try:
            out = subprocess.run(["claude", "-p", "Reply with exactly: ok"],
                                 capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120)
            ok = out.returncode == 0 and "ok" in out.stdout.lower()
            rep.check(ok, "auth + first command round-tripped (`claude -p`)",
                      f"`claude -p` failed (exit {out.returncode}): "
                      f"{(out.stderr or out.stdout).strip()[:200]}")
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            rep.fail("`claude -p` timed out — check network/auth")
        except (subprocess.SubprocessError, OSError) as exc:
            rep.fail(f"`claude -p` could not run ({exc})")
    else:
        rep.pending("skipped auth/first-command probe (--no-probe)")

    # 4. tooling deps
    try:
        import yaml  # noqa: F401
        import jsonschema  # noqa: F401
        rep.ok("course tooling deps present (python, pyyaml, jsonschema)")
    except ImportError as exc:
        rep.fail(f"missing tooling dependency: {exc.name} (pip install pyyaml jsonschema)")

    code = rep.done()
    if code or "--manual" in argv:
        print(MANUAL_CHECKLIST)
    return code


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

aiTitle: Review merge timing and cleanup tasks

content.text:
Good questions. Let me answer the activation one directly, then check two integration points before I build so the fix is consistent.

**On activation:** Once we switch to a project-root `.venv`, the cleanest setup is that **you won't need to activate anything before launching the REPL.** I'll make the Makefile auto-detect `.venv/bin/python` (falling back to `python3`), so `make check` and all the tools find the venv on their own, and I'll call `.venv/bin/python` explicitly for any ad-hoc Python. Relying on a *forgotten* manual activation is exactly what produced today's `exit 127` cascade — so removing that dependency is the actual fix. The only rule: drive Python through `make` or `.venv/bin/python`, not a bare `python3` (which would hit system Python without the deps).

Let me confirm the pre-commit hook and CI invoke things consistently before I change anything.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Local enforcement hook (R13.AC6): run the course's check suite before every commit so
# authoring problems are caught immediately, not in CI.
#
# Activate once per clone:   git config core.hooksPath .githooks
# Skip in an emergency:      git commit --no-verify   (CI is the backstop)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

if command -v make >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "pre-commit: running make check…"
  make check
else
  echo "pre-commit: make not found; running checks directly…"
  for c in check-frontmatter check-coverage check-links check-version-refs check-traceability; do
    python3 "tools/$c"
  done
fi

file.content:
# CI backstop for the course's enforcement suite (R13.AC6). Mirrors the local git pre-commit
# hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so nothing merges that the authoring guard would have rejected.
# This workflow is itself the worked example unit U16 (automate & scale / CI) references.
name: checks

on:
  push:
    branches: ["**"]
  pull_request:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * 1"   # weekly: a trigger to prompt version-drift review (R12.AC7)
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  checks:
    name: enforcement suite
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - name: Install tooling dependencies
        run: python -m pip install --quiet pyyaml jsonschema
      - name: make check
        run: make check

  version-drift:
    name: version drift (informational)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - name: Install tooling dependencies
        run: python -m pip install --quiet pyyaml jsonschema
      - name: Check CLI version drift
        # Best-effort: drift needs the `claude` CLI. If it isn't installed on the runner, this
        # step explains how a maintainer wires the install rather than failing the build.
        run: |
          if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            python tools/check-version-drift --strict
            python tools/render-cli-reference --check --cli   # machine-freshness vs installed CLI (R16.AC6)
          else
            echo "::notice::claude CLI not installed on this runner; run 'make drift' locally"
            echo "where the CLI is available to surface new/removed commands (R12.AC6)."
          fi

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""doctor — the course preflight (R11.AC2).

Checks, in order, and reports pass/fail for each:
  1. install        — the `claude` CLI is on PATH
  2. version        — `claude --version` parses; compared to the recorded verified version
  3. auth + first   — a real non-interactive command round-trips (`claude -p`), proving auth works
  4. tooling deps   — the course's own check suite can run (python + pyyaml + jsonschema in .venv)

If the Python tooling deps are missing, the doctor explains how to create the project venv (.venv)
with either uv or pip — or creates it for you with `--setup-venv`. Once .venv exists, `make`
auto-detects .venv/bin/python, so there is no manual activation step.

It doubles as a worked example of *scripting Claude Code* (R14.AC2): note how step 3 drives the
CLI headlessly with `-p` and inspects the result — the same pattern U16 generalizes.

Usage:
  tools/doctor                    # run all checks (step 3 makes one real API call)
  tools/doctor --no-probe         # skip the API round-trip (offline / CI-safe)
  tools/doctor --manual           # also print the manual checklist fallback
  tools/doctor --setup-venv       # create .venv + install deps (uv if available, else pip)
  tools/doctor --setup-venv --pip # force pip for the venv setup
"""
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
VENV_DIR = REPO_ROOT / ".venv"
VENV_PY = VENV_DIR / "bin" / "python"
TOOLING_DEPS = ("pyyaml", "jsonschema")   # imported as: yaml, jsonschema

MANUAL_CHECKLIST = """
Manual checklist (if the doctor can't run, or to self-verify):
  [ ] `claude --version` prints a version.
  [ ] `claude` opens an interactive session (then type /exit).
  [ ] You are authenticated (run `claude auth` if not).
  [ ] `claude -p "reply with ok"` prints a reply — proves auth + a first command.
  [ ] The course venv exists at .venv with pyyaml + jsonschema (create it with
      `tools/doctor --setup-venv`, or `make venv`); `make` then auto-detects it.
If `claude --version` differs from meta/version-record.md, see that file: some version-specific
details may have moved (they live in meta/version-data.yaml, referenced as {{vd:key}}).
""".rstrip()

VENV_HELP = """\
Set up the course's Python virtualenv at .venv (one time), then re-run the doctor:

  Using uv (fast):
    uv venv .venv
    uv pip install --python .venv/bin/python pyyaml jsonschema

  Using pip (standard library):
    python3 -m venv .venv
    .venv/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip pyyaml jsonschema

Or let the doctor do it:
    tools/doctor --setup-venv         # uses uv if available, otherwise pip
    tools/doctor --setup-venv --pip   # force pip

Once .venv exists, `make` auto-detects .venv/bin/python — you do not need to activate it.
""".rstrip()


def _run(cmd):
    """Echo and run a setup command, returning its exit code."""
    print("  $ " + " ".join(str(c) for c in cmd))
    return subprocess.run(cmd).returncode


def setup_venv(prefer_pip=False):
    """Create .venv (if absent) and install the course tooling deps.

    Stdlib-only so it runs before any dependency exists (the bootstrap case). Prefers uv unless
    --pip is given; falls back to pip + the standard-library `venv` module otherwise.
    """
    uv = shutil.which("uv")
    fresh = not VENV_PY.exists()
    if not fresh:
        print(f"doctor: .venv already exists at {VENV_DIR}; refreshing tooling deps")
    if uv and not prefer_pip:
        rc = _run([uv, "venv", VENV_DIR]) if fresh else 0
        if rc == 0:
            rc = _run([uv, "pip", "install", "--python", VENV_PY, *TOOLING_DEPS])
    else:
        rc = _run([sys.executable, "-m", "venv", VENV_DIR]) if fresh else 0
        if rc == 0:
            rc = _run([VENV_PY, "-m", "pip", "install", "--upgrade", "pip", *TOOLING_DEPS])
    if rc == 0:
        print(f"\ndoctor: .venv ready at {VENV_DIR}. `make` auto-detects it — no activation "
              "needed. Re-run `tools/doctor` (or `make doctor`) to finish the preflight.")
    else:
        print("\ndoctor: venv setup failed; run the commands above manually.")
    return rc


def _ver_tuple(v):
    return tuple(int(x) for x in v.split("."))


def main(argv):
    if "--setup-venv" in argv:
        return setup_venv(prefer_pip="--pip" in argv)

    # The full preflight needs the course tooling deps (pyyaml backs _common). If they aren't
    # importable we can't load _common at all — so guide venv setup first, then bail (re-running
    # the doctor after `--setup-venv` resumes the full check).
    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    try:
        from _common import Reporter, load_version_data
    except ImportError as exc:
        print(f"doctor: course tooling dependency missing ({getattr(exc, 'name', None) or exc}).\n")
        print(VENV_HELP)
        return 1

    rep = Reporter("doctor")
    probe = "--no-probe" not in argv
    recorded = load_version_data().get("_verified_version")

    # 1. install
    claude = shutil.which("claude")
    if not rep.check(bool(claude), f"claude is installed ({claude})", "claude not found on PATH"):
        print(MANUAL_CHECKLIST)
        return rep.done()

    # 2. version
    installed = None
    try:
        out = subprocess.run(["claude", "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
        m = re.search(r"\d+\.\d+\.\d+", out.stdout)
        installed = m.group(0) if m else None
    except (subprocess.SubprocessError, OSError) as exc:
        rep.fail(f"could not run `claude --version` ({exc})")
    if installed:
        if installed == recorded:
            rep.ok(f"version {installed} (matches recorded verified version)")
        elif _ver_tuple(installed) < _ver_tuple(recorded):
            rep.pending(f"version {installed} is older than the verified {recorded}; "
                        "some version-specific details may differ (see meta/version-record.md)")
        else:
            rep.ok(f"version {installed} (newer than verified {recorded}; latest-targeting)")
    else:
        rep.fail("could not parse `claude --version`")

    # 3. auth + first command
    if probe:
        try:
            out = subprocess.run(["claude", "-p", "Reply with exactly: ok"],
                                 capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120)
            ok = out.returncode == 0 and "ok" in out.stdout.lower()
            rep.check(ok, "auth + first command round-tripped (`claude -p`)",
                      f"`claude -p` failed (exit {out.returncode}): "
                      f"{(out.stderr or out.stdout).strip()[:200]}")
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            rep.fail("`claude -p` timed out — check network/auth")
        except (subprocess.SubprocessError, OSError) as exc:
            rep.fail(f"`claude -p` could not run ({exc})")
    else:
        rep.pending("skipped auth/first-command probe (--no-probe)")

    # 4. tooling deps (+ project venv)
    try:
        import yaml  # noqa: F401
        import jsonschema  # noqa: F401
        where = f"in {VENV_DIR.name}/" if sys.prefix == str(VENV_DIR) else "(active environment)"
        rep.ok(f"course tooling deps present (python, pyyaml, jsonschema) {where}")
    except ImportError as exc:
        rep.fail(f"missing tooling dependency: {exc.name}")
        print(VENV_HELP)
    if not VENV_PY.exists():
        rep.pending(f"no project venv at {VENV_DIR.name}/ — create it with "
                    "`tools/doctor --setup-venv` (or `make venv`); `make` then auto-detects it")

    code = rep.done()
    if code or "--manual" in argv:
        print(MANUAL_CHECKLIST)
    return code


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

# Course enforcement suite. `make check` runs the required checks (R13.AC4) plus traceability
# (R13.AC5); it is the same suite wired into the git pre-commit hook and CI (R13.AC6).
#
# PY can be overridden (e.g. `make check PY=python`). The repo's virtualenv lives at .venv.

PY ?= python3
TOOLS := ./tools

.PHONY: check check-strict frontmatter coverage checklist units index links version-refs \
        cli-reference changelog traceability drift doctor render snapshot help

## Run the required + traceability checks (PENDING items do not fail; see check-strict).
check: frontmatter coverage checklist units index links version-refs cli-reference changelog traceability
	@echo "make check: all checks passed"

## Release gate: same suite, but PENDING (not-yet-authored coverage) becomes FAIL.
check-strict: frontmatter coverage checklist units index links version-refs cli-reference changelog
	@$(TOOLS)/check-traceability --strict
	@echo "make check-strict: all checks passed (strict)"

frontmatter:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-frontmatter

coverage:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-coverage

## Confirm course/progress-checklist.md is in sync with the capability map (R9.AC5).
checklist:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-checklist --check

## Confirm each learner-facing unit.md matches render(unit.src.md) — the {{vd:key}} drift gate (R12.AC2).
units:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-units --check

## Confirm the units index (course/units/README.md) is in sync with unit front matter (R9.AC2 navigation).
index:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-index --check

links:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-links

version-refs:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-version-refs

## Offline gate (R16.AC6): cli-reference.json valid vs schema + the page in sync with it.
cli-reference:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-cli-reference --check

## R17.AC5: the recorded verified version has a matching version-changelog.md entry.
changelog:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-version-changelog

traceability:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-traceability

## Drift is informational; run on demand or on a schedule (R12.AC7). Also re-introspects the
## installed CLI to confirm cli-reference.json is fresh (the --check --cli machine-freshness path).
drift:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/check-version-drift
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-cli-reference --check --cli

## Learner preflight (makes one real `claude -p` call unless --no-probe).
doctor:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/doctor

## Verify all {{vd:key}} references in course/ resolve; refresh generated learner docs.
render:
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-vd
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-checklist
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-units
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-index
	@$(PY) $(TOOLS)/render-cli-reference --render

## Refresh the CLI command snapshot used by drift detection.
snapshot:
	@claude --help | awk '/^Commands:/{f=1;next} f && /^  [a-z]/{n=$$1; sub(/\|.*/,"",n); print n}' | sort > meta/cli-commands.snapshot
	@echo "wrote meta/cli-commands.snapshot"

help:
	@grep -B1 -E '^[a-z][a-zA-Z-]+:' Makefile | grep -E '^(##|[a-z])' | sed 'N;s/\n/ /;s/## //'

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""doctor — the course preflight (R11.AC2).

Checks, in order, and reports pass/fail for each:
  1. install        — the `claude` CLI is on PATH
  2. version        — `claude --version` parses; compared to the recorded verified version
  3. auth + first   — a real non-interactive command round-trips (`claude -p`), proving auth works
  4. tooling deps   — the course's own check suite can run (python + pyyaml + jsonschema in .venv)

If the Python tooling deps are missing, the doctor explains how to create the project venv (.venv)
with either uv or pip — or creates it for you with `--setup-venv`. Once .venv exists, `make`
auto-detects .venv/bin/python, so there is no manual activation step.

It doubles as a worked example of *scripting Claude Code* (R14.AC2): note how step 3 drives the
CLI headlessly with `-p` and inspects the result — the same pattern U16 generalizes.

Usage:
  tools/doctor                    # run all checks (step 3 makes one real API call)
  tools/doctor --no-probe         # skip the API round-trip (offline / CI-safe)
  tools/doctor --manual           # also print the manual checklist fallback
  tools/doctor --setup-venv       # create .venv + install deps (uv if available, else pip)
  tools/doctor --setup-venv --pip # force pip for the venv setup
"""
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
VENV_DIR = REPO_ROOT / ".venv"
VENV_PY = VENV_DIR / "bin" / "python"
TOOLING_DEPS = ("pyyaml", "jsonschema")   # imported as: yaml, jsonschema

MANUAL_CHECKLIST = """
Manual checklist (if the doctor can't run, or to self-verify):
  [ ] `claude --version` prints a version.
  [ ] `claude` opens an interactive session (then type /exit).
  [ ] You are authenticated (run `claude auth` if not).
  [ ] `claude -p "reply with ok"` prints a reply — proves auth + a first command.
  [ ] The course venv exists at .venv with pyyaml + jsonschema (create it with
      `tools/doctor --setup-venv`, or `make venv`); `make` then auto-detects it.
If `claude --version` differs from meta/version-record.md, see that file: some version-specific
details may have moved (they live in meta/version-data.yaml, referenced as {{vd:key}}).
""".rstrip()

VENV_HELP = """\
Set up the course's Python virtualenv at .venv (one time), then re-run the doctor:

  Using uv (fast):
    uv venv .venv
    uv pip install --python .venv/bin/python pyyaml jsonschema

  Using pip (standard library):
    python3 -m venv .venv
    .venv/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip pyyaml jsonschema

Or let the doctor do it:
    tools/doctor --setup-venv         # uses uv if available, otherwise pip
    tools/doctor --setup-venv --pip   # force pip

Once .venv exists, `make` auto-detects .venv/bin/python — you do not need to activate it.
""".rstrip()


def _run(cmd):
    """Echo and run a setup command, returning its exit code."""
    print("  $ " + " ".join(str(c) for c in cmd))
    return subprocess.run(cmd).returncode


def setup_venv(prefer_pip=False):
    """Create .venv (if absent) and install the course tooling deps.

    Stdlib-only so it runs before any dependency exists (the bootstrap case). Prefers uv unless
    --pip is given; falls back to pip + the standard-library `venv` module otherwise.
    """
    uv = shutil.which("uv")
    fresh = not VENV_PY.exists()
    if not fresh:
        print(f"doctor: .venv already exists at {VENV_DIR}; refreshing tooling deps")
    if uv and not prefer_pip:
        rc = _run([uv, "venv", VENV_DIR]) if fresh else 0
        if rc == 0:
            rc = _run([uv, "pip", "install", "--python", VENV_PY, *TOOLING_DEPS])
    else:
        rc = _run([sys.executable, "-m", "venv", VENV_DIR]) if fresh else 0
        if rc == 0:
            rc = _run([VENV_PY, "-m", "pip", "install", "--upgrade", "pip", *TOOLING_DEPS])
    if rc == 0:
        print(f"\ndoctor: .venv ready at {VENV_DIR}. `make` auto-detects it — no activation "
              "needed. Re-run `tools/doctor` (or `make doctor`) to finish the preflight.")
    else:
        print("\ndoctor: venv setup failed; run the commands above manually.")
    return rc


def _ver_tuple(v):
    return tuple(int(x) for x in v.split("."))


def main(argv):
    if "--setup-venv" in argv:
        return setup_venv(prefer_pip="--pip" in argv)

    # The full preflight needs the course tooling deps (pyyaml backs _common). If they aren't
    # importable we can't load _common at all — so guide venv setup first, then bail (re-running
    # the doctor after `--setup-venv` resumes the full check).
    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    try:
        from _common import Reporter, load_version_data
    except ImportError as exc:
        print(f"doctor: course tooling dependency missing ({getattr(exc, 'name', None) or exc}).\n")
        print(VENV_HELP)
        return 1

    rep = Reporter("doctor")
    probe = "--no-probe" not in argv
    recorded = load_version_data().get("_verified_version")

    # 1. install
    claude = shutil.which("claude")
    if not rep.check(bool(claude), f"claude is installed ({claude})", "claude not found on PATH"):
        print(MANUAL_CHECKLIST)
        return rep.done()

    # 2. version
    installed = None
    try:
        out = subprocess.run(["claude", "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
        m = re.search(r"\d+\.\d+\.\d+", out.stdout)
        installed = m.group(0) if m else None
    except (subprocess.SubprocessError, OSError) as exc:
        rep.fail(f"could not run `claude --version` ({exc})")
    if installed:
        if installed == recorded:
            rep.ok(f"version {installed} (matches recorded verified version)")
        elif _ver_tuple(installed) < _ver_tuple(recorded):
            rep.pending(f"version {installed} is older than the verified {recorded}; "
                        "some version-specific details may differ (see meta/version-record.md)")
        else:
            rep.ok(f"version {installed} (newer than verified {recorded}; latest-targeting)")
    else:
        rep.fail("could not parse `claude --version`")

    # 3. auth + first command
    if probe:
        try:
            out = subprocess.run(["claude", "-p", "Reply with exactly: ok"],
                                 capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120)
            ok = out.returncode == 0 and "ok" in out.stdout.lower()
            rep.check(ok, "auth + first command round-tripped (`claude -p`)",
                      f"`claude -p` failed (exit {out.returncode}): "
                      f"{(out.stderr or out.stdout).strip()[:200]}")
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            rep.fail("`claude -p` timed out — check network/auth")
        except (subprocess.SubprocessError, OSError) as exc:
            rep.fail(f"`claude -p` could not run ({exc})")
    else:
        rep.pending("skipped auth/first-command probe (--no-probe)")

    # 4. tooling deps (+ project venv)
    try:
        import yaml  # noqa: F401
        import jsonschema  # noqa: F401
        where = f"in {VENV_DIR.name}/" if sys.prefix == str(VENV_DIR) else "(active environment)"
        rep.ok(f"course tooling deps present (python, pyyaml, jsonschema) {where}")
    except ImportError as exc:
        rep.fail(f"missing tooling dependency: {exc.name}")
        print(VENV_HELP)
    if not VENV_PY.exists():
        rep.pending(f"no project venv at {VENV_DIR.name}/ — create it with "
                    "`tools/doctor --setup-venv` (or `make venv`); `make` then auto-detects it")

    code = rep.done()
    if code or "--manual" in argv:
        print(MANUAL_CHECKLIST)
    return code


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
